departments.name: Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery
departments.description: The Department of Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery plays a vital role in supporting the Surgical Oncology team, especially in Head and Neck cancers, Breast cancers, Skin cancers, and Soft Tissue tumours of the limbs, abdomen, and chest wall. The department has performed the highest number of microvascular reconstructions in Chhattisgarh over the last 7 years. It maintains an impressive success rate of over 99%, with a very low rate of re-exploration. It also leads the region in breast reconstruction surgeries, offering excellent aesthetic results. The department uses advanced equipment like the Leica Provido Operating Microscope with integrated ICG counter, which helps in performing complex microsurgeries and super-microsurgeries with precision. Recently, the department has expanded beyond oncological reconstructions to include a wide range of plastic and aesthetic procedures such as: • Scar management • Skin tag and mole removal • Hair loss treatment (medical and surgical) • Rhinoplasty (for cosmetic and functional reasons) • Abdominoplasty (tummy tuck) and liposuction • Breast augmentation and reduction • Fat grafting • Non-surgical aesthetic treatments like Botox and fillers • Burn care (OPD basis) • Hand surgeries (claw hand, trigger finger, tendon repairs, etc.) To support these services, the department is equipped with modern tools like: • Liposuction machine • Radiofrequency ablation system • Microneedling devices • Centrifuge units for PRP and fat grafting With this growth, the Department of Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery at BALCO Medical Centre is now offering comprehensive surgical and aesthetic care to both cancer and non-cancer patients.

departments.name: Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery
departments.description: The Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery department at BALCO Medical Centre plays a key role in the comprehensive management of head and neck cancers. As a specialised cancer centre, our focus is on early diagnosis, precise surgical treatment, and timely reconstruction to help patients regain function and confidence. Our team performs a full spectrum of head and neck oncologic surgeries, including tumour excision, neck dissections, and complex reconstructions using local, regional, and microvascular flaps. We also manage facial trauma, oral pathologies, jaw disorders, and conditions related to cancer treatment, such as osteoradionecrosis, drug induced osteonecrosis. Working closely with medical oncology, radiation oncology, pathology, radiology, and rehabilitation services, we ensure that every patient receives coordinated, evidence-based, and compassionate care. With advanced surgical expertise and a multidisciplinary approach, the department is committed to restoring speech, swallowing, appearance, and quality of life for all patients undergoing oral cancer treatment at BMC.

departments.name: Ortho Oncosurgery
departments.description: The Orthopaedic Oncosurgery department at BALCO Medical Centre specialises in the diagnosis and treatment of bone and soft-tissue tumours. As a dedicated cancer centre, our focus is on providing safe, effective, and limb-preserving surgical solutions for patients with musculoskeletal cancers. Our team manages a wide range of conditions, including primary bone tumours, soft-tissue sarcomas, metastatic bone disease, and complex skeletal reconstruction needs. We perform limb-sparing surgeries, tumour resections, endoprosthetic reconstructions, and stabilisation procedures to relieve pain, restore mobility, and improve quality of life. Working closely with medical oncology, radiation oncology, radiology, pathology, and rehabilitation services, the department ensures coordinated and evidence-based care for every patient. Our approach combines advanced surgical techniques with compassionate support, helping patients return to daily activities with confidence and dignity during and after cancer treatment.

departments.name: Medical Oncology
departments.description: The Medical Oncology department is backed by qualified and experienced medical oncologists and trained resident doctors and nursing staffs in field of medical oncology. Onco daycare at Balco Medical Centre is the largest day care in a private facility in Chhattisgarh with 26 beds. The Department of Medical Oncology offers diagnostics and treatment of most types of cancer, development and implementation of preventive measures programs, and the possibilities of clinical research. It deals with the medicinal part of oncology (except surgery / radiation – rest all are dealt here, all aspects of cancer such as Curative, Palliative, & Supportive treatment). Treatment plans and observations of patients diagnosed with cancer are developed considering the interdisciplinary approach, and in close cooperation with the departments of oncosurgery, radiology, oncopathology, nuclear medicine, therapy, and radiation oncology. Under the guidance of medical specialists, a team of specialized oncospecialist nurses, experienced in meeting the psychological and physical needs of cancer patients, conducts chemotherapy and provides care for cancer patients. The department offers comprehensive medical oncology services including Chemotherapy, Hormone therapy, Targeted & Biological therapy and Immunotherapy. For some types of tumours (e.g. Blood cancer, advance stage solid cancer), chemotherapy is the only possible method of treatment. Some cancers require simultaneous or sequential use of radiotherapy and chemotherapy while others may require chemotherapy sessions before and after surgery. The duration of the course of treatment, as well as the frequency of its use, is determined by a chemotherapy program, individually designed taking into account the patient’s conditions (age, height, weight, BMI, BSA, CBC, Kidney and Liver status, stage and type of cancer).

departments.name: Surgical Oncology
departments.description: Surgical Oncology is a speciality capable of performing a diverse range of oncological surgeries in the areas of Head & Neck cancer, Breast cancer, Soft Tissue Sarcomas, Gastrointestinal, Thoracic malignancies and Gynaecological cancers. The Surgical Oncology department is backed by highly trained and experienced team of surgeons and associated specialised staff in the field of oncology. The Department of Surgical Oncology offers clinical evaluation and diagnostic work-up including biopsies for various types of cancer, treatment planning and surgery to remove the cancer tissues. Surgical oncologists also perform palliative and emergency surgeries and surgeries to stage the disease in certain circumstances. The department boasts of performing complex Head and Neck surgeries for advanced cancers. It has performed the largest number of microvascular reconstructions in Chhattisgarh with highest success rate. The department of surgical oncology at Balco Medical Centre has been credited with many firsts in Chhattisgarh like Supermicrosurgery in Breast Reconstruction, Femur reconstruction with vascularised fibula bone and extracorporeal irradiation of femur autograft, limb preservation in sarcomas, and virtual surgical planning and CAD-CAM based 3D printing. The team commonly performes complex surgeries like Cytoreductive surgeries with HIPEC, Liver resection, Thoracotomy, Pneumonectomy, Mediastinal tumours, Esophagectomy with gastric pull-up, Esophagectomy with Colonic transposition, Laryngo-pharyngo-esophagectomy, organ-conservation surgeries like inter-sphincteric resection for very low rectal cancers, Breast conserving surgery and oncoplasty, Fertility-preserving surgeries like Radical Trachelectomy, and minimally invasive surgeries for gynae-oncological malignancy, colorectal malignancy, Genito-urinary cancers & Laparoscopic adrenalectomy. The surgical oncologists have also conducted successful excision of IVC sarcoma and complex RP sarcomas requiring compartmental resection. The team boasts of successfully performing MRM on a 104-year-old female who was diagnosed with Breast Cancer. The department shortly plans to start robotic surgeries to remove complex and hard to reach tumours, PIPAC for peritoneal carcinomatosis and Supermicrosurgery and Lymphatic Surgery in cases of Lymphedema. Following are the sub-specialities of cancer treated by the Department of Surgical Oncology in Balco Medical Centre: Head and Neck Oral and oropharyngeal cancers, Laryngeal cancers, Esophageal cancers, Nasal and paranasal sinus tumours, Orbital tumours, Skin and scalp tumours, Salivary gland tumours Breast and Endocrine Breast cancers, Benign breast diseases, Thyroid and parathyroid gland tumours, Adrenocortical tumours and paragangliomas Gastrointestinal Complex hepatobiliary pancreatic surgeries including liver resections and pancreatico-duodenectomy, minimal-invasive colorectal cancer surgeries including intersphincteric resection, retroperioneal sarcoma and cancers of major vessels Gynaecological Cytoreductive surgeries and HIPEC for ovarian and primary peritoneal cancers, organ-preserving surgeries and fertility preservation in endometrium and ovarian cancers, minimal-invasive surgeries for endometrial and cervical cancers, cancers of vulva and vagina Uro-oncology Cancers of Kidney, ureter and Urinary Bladder, Cancers of Penis, Prostatic cancers, Continence preserving surgeries like Neobladder reconstruction Diagnostic Procedures Biopsies, UGIE, DL-Scopy, Diagnostic Laparoscopy, Hysteroscopy, Bronchoscopy Bone cancers, Limb preservation surgery Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery Microvascular Reconstruction, Head and Neck Reconstruction, Breast Oncoplasty, Breast Reconstruction, Chest wall Reconstruction, Abdominal wall Reconstruction, Penile Reconstruction, Skin and soft tissue Reconstruction, Bone and extremity Reconstruction, Breast reduction and augmentation, Body contouring, CAD-CAM based 3D printing in bone reconstruction, Secondary Reconstruction and revision in cancer Diagnostic Procedures Biopsies, UGIE, DL-Scopy, departments.name: Radiation Oncology
departments.description: Radiation Oncology or radiotherapy is one of the important modalities used in the treatment of cancer. A wide range of cancers can be treated with radiation therapy. In most cases, radiation therapy is coupled with surgery or chemotherapy for a better clinical outcome. It can be received as an outpatient care. Radiation therapy is a non-invasive or minimally invasive modality that uses high energy rays that destroys the DNA of cancer cells and thus resulting in death or slowing the growth of cancer cells. Advancements in the field of radiation oncology have made it possible for oncologists to treat various cancer types successfully without serious complications. Technology and Services: Considering the importance of radiation oncology in the fight against cancer, Balco Medical Centre has equipped itself with the best in-class equipment in entire Central India and offers the following services: External Beam Radiation Therapy:  State-of-the-art Varian TrueBeam system (Linear Accelerator) that integrates respiratory gating, onboard imaging and treatment delivery enabling a range of treatment techniques such as: Three Dimensional Conformal Radiation Therapy (3DCRT) Intensity Modulated Radiation Therapy (IMRT) Image-Guided Radiation Therapy (IGRT) Stereotactic Radio Surgery (SRS) and Radiotherapy (SRT) Stereotactic Body Radiotherapy (SBRT) High-Dose Rate Brachytherapy: Using GammaMedplus iX (24-Channels) involves the use of radioactive sources that are either implanted into the tumour (interstitial brachytherapy) or placed near it, generally in a body cavity (intracavitary brachytherapy) enabling precise treatment. Availability of TrueBeam and GammaMedplus iX makes us a full-fledged Radiation Oncology Department. The Department has been networked to CT, MRI, SPECT CT and PET CT and our proficient doctors can fuse these images to accurately target the smallest of the tumours while sparing surrounding normal organs. The department has a complete team of highly trained & experienced Radiation Oncologists, Medical Physicists, Technologists, Nursing and supporting Staff who are providing comprehensive care and treatment. Our Experts Dr. Gourav Gupta Radiation Oncology Book an appointment Dr. Gourav Gupta Dr. Rajendra Prasad Patel Radiation Oncology Book an appointment Dr. Rajendra Prasad Patel Dr. Yusuf Malik Radiation Oncology Dr. Yusuf Malik Dr. Prabhakar Gupta Radiation Oncology Book an appointment Dr. Prabhakar Gupta

departments.name: Haematology
departments.description: Dept of Hematology, Hemato – Oncology and Bone marrow Transpalnt Hematology is a branch of medicine concerning the study of blood, the blood-forming organs, and blood diseases and blood cancer. The Department of Haematology & Hemato - Oncology provides comprehensive clinical and laboratory services to all patients with irregularities of blood components and blood cancer. Diagnostic Hematology State-of-the-art Hematopathology lab with 5 part cell counter Bone marrow aspiration and biopsy facility In-house Flowcytometry for rapid diagnosis of Leukemia reporting within 24 hour Advanced Immunohistochemistry to characterize lymphoma subtype and myeloma Facility for molecular genetic study In-house CD 34 assay for stem cell enumeration HLA typing and other immune hematology services Ancilliary supportive lab services for Bone marrow transplant Diagnostic facility for anemia and bleeding and coagulation disorder Benign Hematology Diagnostic service and treatment facility for different anemia, e.g.: aplastic anemia, iron deficiency, myelodysplasia Special thalassemia and sickle cell anemia clinic for multidisciplinary treatment Day care blood transfusion and exchange transfusion facility Immunotherapy for ITP and Autoimmune hemolytic anemia Hemophilia clinic for factor infusion therapy and supportive care Malignant Hematology Treatment for different types of blood cancer (acute and chronic leukemia) Pediatric hematology for Childhood leukemia treatment Chemotherapy facility and Infusional chemotherapy at day care Immunotherapy & Targeted therapy Oral therapy for blood cancer Newer developmental molecules and trial therapy for relapse and refractory patient Second opinion facility through walk in and video consultation Stem cell transplant The stem cell transplant is a highly sophisticated procedure whereby donor’s normal stem cell are collected and infused into patient’s body. This is the only centre in Chhattisgarh to provide both autologous and allogenic stem cell transplant procedure. Autologous stem cell transplant for Myeloma and lymphoma Allogenic stem cell transplant (both matched and mismatched) for all leukemia, aplastic anemia Haplo-identical ( Half-matched ) stem cell transplant Pediatric allogenic stem cell transplant Across blood group stem cell transplant Stem cell cryopreservation and storage facility Post-transplant follow up clinic Procurement of stem cell from donor registry where matched donor not available Our Experts Dr. Dibyendu De Haemato Oncology Book an appointment Dr. Dibyendu De

departments.name: Paediatric Oncology
departments.description: Childhood cancers are not the same as adult cancers and a child is definitely not a young adult. Children’s body and the way they respond to treatment are unique. Malignancies like leukaemia, lymphoma and solid tumours are quite common in children. Children with these conditions have special physical, emotional, nutritional and psychological needs. Childhood cancers needs to be treated in a child-friendly atmosphere by a dedicated team. They require the highest level of medical expertise and highly specialized medical treatment combined with very personalized support from their doctor and caring staff. Paediatric oncologists are the experts who treat children with blood cancer and cancer of other body parts. Special attributes BALCO Medical Centre is the only hospital in Chhattisgarh with a full-time paediatric oncologist and a dedicated paediatric oncology unit. There is a dedicated 8-bedded paediatric ward along with day care department. We are committed to build an overall positive experience of children and their families and support them in every possible way in their journey to fight with cancer. Our doctors and staff are trained and experienced in supporting children during treatment for serious illnesses. Their skill and compassion can make all the difference for a child and family and help in renewing health and restoring hope. Tata Medical Centre – BALCO Medical Centre has an association with TMC Mumbai, whereby we discuss our complicated cases with them in national cancer tumour board, if required, for second opinion. We can also guide and refer the families to TMH Mumbai, if more advanced tertiary care is required. Paediatric Oncology OPD every Wednesday : Ekta Children’s hospital: 9.30 am to 12.00 pm Bal Gopal Children’s Hospital: 1.00 pm to 3.30 pm After Completion of treatment (ACT) clinic – Follow-up clinic to monitor wellbeing and any long-term side-effects post completion of treatment. Paediatric dietician from Cuddles Foundation – Unlike adults, nutrition plays an important role in treatment of children with cancer. Lack of adequate nutrition can have a negative effect on cancer treatment with immediate and long term side effects. We are fortunate to have a dedicated paediatric dietician to support children nutrition. Play area - We have a dedicated play area for children to engage them in various activities during their prolonged hospital stay. Indradhanush is our paediatric patient support group, where we communicate with parents to answer their queries as much as possible. Through this group, we organise regular activities for children and create awareness amongst parents. “SMILE “is our annual childhood cancer survivorship programme to celebrate the life of our little warriors. Financial Constrains – Last but not the least cancer can come as a big financial burden for families as parents have to stay with children and often loose work. Treatment of cancer can be prolonged, expensive and if complicated cannot always be supported by Govt. schemes alone. Sometimes families are so poor that they cannot even afford initial investigations to make the diagnosis. We believe that every child has the right to treatment. We have some special funds to initiate investigations and to help very poor families. We also try to raise funds through NGOs to help us support them. BMC offers treatment of all childhood cancers including: • Leukaemia • Lymphoma – Hodgkin’s and Non-Hodgkin’s • Wilms’ tumour, other kidney tumors • Hepatoblastoma • Neuroblastoma • Soft tissue sarcoma • Bone tumors • Rare germ cell tumors • Retinoblastoma • Bone marrow transplant Our Experts Dr. Ruchi Aujla Paediatric Oncology Book an appointment Dr. Ruchi Aujla Dr. Srishti Srivastava Paediatric Oncology Book an appointment Dr. Srishti Srivastava

departments.name: Nuclear Medicine
departments.description: Nuclear medicine is a multidisciplinary field that develops and uses instrumentation and tracers (radiopharmaceuticals) to study physiological processes and noninvasively diagnose, stage, and treat diseases. Particularly, it offers a unique means to study cancer biology in vivo and to optimize cancer therapy for individual patients. Nuclear medicine imaging, including single-photon emission computer tomography (SPECT) and positron emission tomography (PET-CT), can provide important functional information and quantitative about normal tissues or disease conditions, in contrast to conventional, anatomical imaging techniques. The role of nuclear medicine in cancer therapy involves use of tumor-targeting agents, conjugated with therapeutic radionuclides, used to deposit lethal radiation at tumor & metastatic sites. The Nuclear Medicine department is backed by highly trained and experienced Nuclear Medicine Physicians and Physicist/RSO specialised in the field of Nuclear Medicine. It is equipped with 68Ga generator that helps in-house radiopharmaceutical formulation of 68Ga-PSMA/DOTA. The department has successfully started 177-Lu PSMA Therapy for the first time in Central India. Diagnostic Procedures Conducted : SPECT-CT: Bone Scan, Renal Scan, Thyroid Scan, Iodine scan, Cardiac Scan, Para Thyroid Scan, Sentinel Node Mapping, Lung Ventilation/Perfusion Scan, Brain Scan, Liver Scan, Hepatobiliary Scan, GI Bleed Scan, Meckel’s Diverticulum Scan, Gastric Emptying Scan, Salivary Gland Scan, Lymphoscintigraphy, Bone Marrow Imaging, Infection/Inflammation Imaging. PET-CT: WB PET CT Scan (18F-FDG & 68Ga-PSMA/DOTA) Cardiac PET CT Scan Therapeutic Procedures: Lutetium (177Lu-PSMA/DOTA) Therapy – For Carcinoma Prostate /Neuroendocrine tumour High dose Radioiodine Therapy (131-I) – for Well differentiated thyroid carcinoma Low dose Radioiodine Therapy (131-I) – for treating Hyperactive thyroid gland 131-I MIBG Therapy – for Neuroblastoma Bone Pain Palliation Therapy (153-Sm, 177Lu-EDTMP) Our Experts Dr. Jay Kumar Rai Nuclear Medicine Book an appointment Dr. Jay Kumar Rai Dr. Vivek Baghel Nuclear Medicine Book an appointment Dr. Vivek Baghel

departments.name: Pain & Palliative Care
departments.description: At Balco Medical Centre, our palliative care experts look after both acute pain management and chronic cancer pain in OPD, IPD and Emergency. Our consultants, senior residents and staff nurses effectively provide the service to the needy patients round the clock. The acute pain service looks after post-operative pain after major surgeries and all other acute and acute on chronic pain conditions. Chronic Cancer pain is managed using a multimodal approach, ranging from the use of oral analgesics including NSAIDs, co-analgesics, various forms of morphine, transdermal fentanyl patch to neurolytic nerve blocks given under ultrasound & fluoroscopic guidance. The hospital has designated Pain & Palliative Care OPD Clinic, where treatment is offered for a variety of symptoms affecting those patients with advanced cancer. Our Experts Dr. Nilima Das Pain and Palliative Care Dr. Nilima Das

departments.name: Anesthesia & Intensive Care
departments.description: The Department of Anaesthesia & Critical Care in Balco Medical Centre, Naya Raipur provides multidisciplinary services to the patients by our excellent team of specialists. This is an integrated service department which provides the best possible care with the help of modern and efficient techniques, skills and knowledge to ensure complete patient safety, comfort and satisfaction in perioperative & pain management along with critical care. During the COVID pandemic, the critically ill patients were efficiently managed by the department. Facilities Intensive Care Unit Balco Medical Centre’s 35-bedded ICU is well- equipped with modern ventilators, High-flow nasal cannula (HFNC), multiparameter monitors, ultrasonography and echocardiography machine. The department serves critically-ill surgical patients, patients with acute medical illness, co-morbid conditions and patients with haematological malignancies, neutropenia and chemotherapy-related complications in SICU, MICU and isolation. These patients have complex problems like Sepsis, Acute respiratory distress syndrome (ARDS), and multi-organ failure, many having acute haemodynamic, respiratory and metabolic derangements. The department is equipped with super isolation rooms for providing better supportive care and management for the vulnerable patients. The unit is having well-trained intensivists and nursing staffs to provide the entire range of intensive care services including airway management, mechanical ventilation, circulatory support including advanced haemodynamic monitoring, enteral /parenteral nutrition, blood component therapy, sedation, pain relief and other supportive measures. The ICU has several quality assurance programmes and various protocols have been implemented to evaluate and improve the quality of care in the ICU. Anaesthesia & Perioperative Care The Anaesthesia service is involved with the peri-operative management of patients undergoing cancer surgery in the modular operation theatre complex for routine and emergency procedures, as well as in peripheral areas including emergency, radiotherapy, brachytherapy, endoscopy and radiology. Our modular OT complex consists of 5 operating rooms, equipped with modern machines and equipment like Workstations, Fibreoptic bronchoscope and CMAC (Videolaryngoscope) for managing difficult Airways, Ultrasound and Fluoroscopy. The anaesthetic management of patients undergoing extensive cancer surgery offers challenging problems including the management of difficult airway, acute haemorrhage, massive blood transfusion, anaesthesia for emergency surgery in patients with severe sepsis and haemorrhage. Our qualified and skilled anaesthetists administer anaesthesia to these patients with monitored anaesthesia care, performing invasive procedures and haemodynamic monitoring. Post-op Recovery All patients after major or minor surgical procedures are shifted to the recovery room for observation before being transferred to the ward. During this period they are monitored by a team of anaesthetists and nurses, who perform an initial post-operative evaluation, pain management, manage any acute post-operative event and also decide when the patient is ready for transfer. Pre-operative Clinic Balco Medical Centre has designated PAC clinic to conduct pre-operative anaesthetic evaluation of patients planned for surgery. A team of anaesthetists evaluate the patient's medical condition and formulate an anaesthetic plan, taking into account the patient's physical condition. This is then discussed with the patient, including the risks involved and details of any procedures likely to be performed in the peri-operative period. Emergency Care Balco Medical Centre provides 24X7 service in the ER for the patients with Palliative care emergencies, Acute pain and Critically & terminally-ill patients. Our Experts Dr. Alok Kumar Swain Intensive Care Dr. Alok Kumar Swain Dr. Santosh Tharwani Intensive Care Book an appointment Dr. Santosh Tharwani Dr. Shilpa L Meshram Intensive Care Book an appointment Dr. Shilpa L Meshram Dr. Devendra Kumar Intensive Care Dr. Devendra Kumar Dr. Devavrat Vaishnav Intensive Care Dr. Devavrat Vaishnav Dr. Subhendu Mishra Intensive Care Dr. Subhendu Mishra

departments.name: Laboratory Services
departments.description: Laboratory services at Balco Medical Centre constitutes of two sub-departments, Department of Oncopathology and Department of Microbiology. Department of Oncopathology Early and accurate diagnosis is key to the accurate and effective treatment of cancer and oncopathology services provide the diagnostic backbone for oncologists. The department has in- house advanced pathology services including surgical pathology, frozen section, immunohistochemistry, cytopathology, hematopathology, flow cytometry, and clinical biochemistry. Histopathology- Histopathology has a central role in cancer care and pathologist helps in diagnosis of the cancer by studying the tissue, describe various parameters in reports such as grade, margin status etc. to determine the prognosis, provides the final staging of the cancer and also evaluate the response to therapy which help clinicians decide the treatment protocol. Immunohistochemistry (IHC)- Different cancer types have different treatment protocols. IHC is used for exact categorisation of the tumour (carcinoma/lymphoma/ sarcoma), to evaluate prognosis and behaviour of disease as well as in guiding the treatment (ER, PR, HER-2 neu, ALK, MSI, etc.). We are first private hospital in CG state to start in-house immunohistochemistry test with approx. 130 antibodies on board. Frozen section help surgeons for Rapid Intra- Op diagnosis and margin assessment. Cytopathology section deals with FNAC, fluid cytology, cell block preparation, PAP smears and rapid onsite adequacy checking in image-guided biopsy/ FNAC. Hematology section deals with routine blood counts, peripheral smear examination, coagulation profile and clinical pathology. We have first in- house flow cytometry for diagnosis of leukaemia and CD34 stem cell enumeration. The department is planning to start MRD panels in near future. Biochemistry- We have a dry chemistry platform for analysis of routine biochemistry parameters and we are also performing tumour marker assays to help clinicians. We have a dedicated and experienced team of Oncopathologists trained at India’s premier institutes like TMH, Mumbai, KEMH Mumbai and PGI Chandigarh handling the above departments and providing accurate reports with faster turnaround time. Pathologists are also an active members of Multidisciplinary tumour boards contributing in the Diagnosis, Predictive and prognostic outcomes and thus have a role in comprehensive patient care. We also have state of the art equipment and more than that, trained and skilled technicians to handle them. Services offered: 1.Histopathology Small Biopsies Medium Specimens Large/ Radical specimens Immunohistochemistry Frozen section Special stains (AFB, PAS, GMS, Mucicarmne, Alcian Blue, Congo Red etc.) 2.Cytopathology FNAC Fluid Cytology for Malignant cells Cervical Pap smears CSF cytology Special stain on Fluid/ FNAC Cell Block preparation Rapid onsite Adequacy Checking 3.Biochemistry RFT LFT Electrolytes TFT HbA1C Tumor Markers- CEA, AFP, HCG, CA125, CA19-9, PSA Lipid Profile RBS/Fasting / PP sugar Ferritin D- Dimer Procalcitonin Vitamin D 4.Haematology CBC PS comment PT/INR APTT ESR Sickling test Reticulocyte count Flow Cytometry 5.Clinical Pathology Urine Routine/ Microscopy Fluids- R/M Latest technology/instruments or equipment used & its features Histopathology- Fully Automated Platform from LEICA which is one of the leading company in Histopath tissue processing equipments for Tissue processing. Histopathology has a central role in cancer care and helps in- Immunohistochemistry- Fully automated IHC platform from one of the leading company i.e Ventana Benchmark XT with approx. 130 Antibodies. Frozen section- Cryostat machine from Leica to help surgeons for Rapid Intra- Op diagnosis and Margin assessment Cytopathology- Sakura Cytospin for CSF and Urine Hematology- 6 part Differential counter by Sysmex XN 350, 10 colored Beckmen Coulter- Navios EX Flow Cytometer. Biochemistry- Vitros Dry chemistry, Mini- Vidas, Biorad D-10 Department of Microbiology Department of Microbiology is manned by experienced consultants along with trained, skilled and experienced technical professionals and offers services in the field of Diagnostic Microbiology, Clinical consultation in Infectious diseases and integrated antimicrobial stewardship, Hospital Infection Control and diagnostic stewardship. The department’s diagnostic microbiology facilities include the following sections: Bacteriology, Serology, Mycology, Mycobacteriology, Virology, Parasitology, and Molecular Microbiology. The department also offers services in Environmental Microbiology (testing of water quality and air quality), and sterility testing of various sterile products. Currently, Molecular diagnostic tests offered by the department are accredited to the National Accreditation Board for Testing and Calibration Laboratories (NABL). The department has been felicitated by MoHFW and NHM for implementation of the best practices for antimicrobial, infection control and diagnostic stewardship program. Facilities Biosafety level- 2 and 2+ laboratory facilities Automated blood culture system Automated bacteria and yeast identification and antimicrobial susceptibility testing system Light microscope and fluorescence microscope Deep freezers (minus 20, minus 80 Celsius) Type IIA2 Biosafety cabinets Carbon dioxide, BOD and other incubators Chip based nucleic acid amplification system Automated serology systems Air Particle Counter Water quality monitoring systems: chlorine meter, membrane filtration system for water microbiology Special Procedures / Operations COVID-19 testing, Hepatitis B, Hepatitis C Viral load testing, Mycobacterium species detection and drug resistance testing, HPV virus detection by Real time RT-PCR (Closed system) Our Experts Dr. Sandeep Ojha Histo Pathology Dr. Sandeep Ojha Dr. Shreshtha Tiwari Microbiology Dr. Shreshtha Tiwari Dr. NIKHIL JAIN Histo Pathology Dr. NIKHIL JAIN Dr. Deepshikha Parakh Histo Pathology Dr. Deepshikha Parakh Dr. Arti Gupta Laboratory Services Dr. Arti Gupta

departments.name: Radiology
departments.description: Radiology is the medical discipline that uses medical imaging to diagnose and help to treat disease. It plays a vital role in clinical decision making, treatment response & follow up. Balco Medical Centre’s Radiology Department is well-equipped with all modalities. Since its inception, the department has been instrumental in providing healthcare with advancements in imaging technology, such as CT & MRI. All images are integrated with Picture Archival and Communication System (PACS), making it a filmless and paperless department. The highly trained radiologists, working with state-of-the-art equipment, strive to provide an unparalleled radiology service to patients. The department of radiology offers the following services: Digital X-Ray (Siemens), CR system Digital Mammography (Fujifilm Amulet innovality) Sonography and Colour Doppler – High-end GE Ultrasound machines 64 Slice (Siemens) CT scan MRI 1.5 T (Siemens Amira) Image-guided procedures - CT and ultrasound-guided biopsies, PCN, Paracentesis, pigtail drainage & TRUS-guided biopsy Our Experts Dr. Lade Gaurav Radiology Services Dr. Lade Gaurav Dr. Sateesh Kumar Atmakuri Radiology Services Dr. Sateesh Kumar Atmakuri

departments.name: Transfusion Medicine & Blood Centre
departments.description: State-of-the-art Transfusion Medicine and Blood Centre department of Balco Medical Centre is equipped with the facilities of modern blood banking, 100 % blood components, donor and therapeutic apheresis, Immunohematology, Leucodepletion and Irradiation facilities run by a team of dedicated Transfusion Medicine specialist and technicians. The department has modern blood banking with 100% blood component facility through automated blood component extractor. Available blood components are Packed Red cells, Platelet Concentrate, Fresh frozen plasma, Cryoprecipitate & Buffy Coat. Apheresis Facilities: Donor apheresis: Plateletpheresis for single donor platelet (SDP) collection Plasma apheresis Haematopoitic Stem Cell Apheresis (for Bone marrow transplantation) Leukapheresis (Granulocyte collection) Therapeutic apheresis: Therapeutic plasma exchange (TPE) Therapeutic Leukapheresis Therapeutic Red Cell Exchange (RCE) - Erythrocytapheresis Haematopoitic Stem Cell Apheresis (HSCT) Immunohaematology Services: Red Cell Antigen Phenotyping Antibody screening & identification Coombs testing (DCT & ICT) Resolution of: Incompatible cross match ABO discrepancies Transfusion reactions DCT positive cases Additional services: Facilitation of Intrauterine transfusion (IUT) procedures for severe HDFN (Hemolytic Disease of Fetus and Newborn) Therapeutic phlebotomy Leucoreduction of blood components Irradiation of blood components Our Experts Dr. Neelesh Jain Transfusion Medicine & Blood Centre Book an appointment Dr. Neelesh Jain

departments.name: Internal Medicine
departments.description: Internal Medicine is a parent branch of different superspecialities (Cardiology, Neurology, Nephrology, Endocrinology, and Gastroenterology), therefore, all the oncology patients who are suffering from any of the ailments related to these departments are evaluated and managed at the Department of Internal Medicine. Ailments like Chest Pain, Seizures, Neurologic Deficits, Deranged Renal Functions, Dyselectrolytemia (Hypernatremia, Hyponatremia, Hyperkalemia, Hypokalemia), Diabetes, & Thyroid Dysfunction are managed in OPD as well as in IPD patients. The department is manned by highly trained expert of internal medicine with additional proficiency in non-invasive cardiology and echocardiography. Non-oncology case management: Fever (Malaria, Typhoid, Dengue, Leptospirosis, Scrub Typhus) Bacterial Pneumonia, COVID Pneumonia, Tuberculosis Fluid and Electrolyte Imbalance Anaemia Diabetes, Hypertension, Rheumatoid Arthritis, Hepatitis Snake Bite, Scorpion Sting, Poisoning Diagnostics offered: Electrocardiography Echocardiography TMT Pulmonary Function Test Our Experts Dr. Namita Walter General Medicine Book an appointment Dr. Namita Walter

departments.name: Allied Services
departments.description: Allied Services For complete and holistic recovery of patients, Balco Medical Centre offers allied services like dietary services, psychological counselling, dental, physiotherapy, speech & swallowing therapy and rehabilitation services. Dietary Services Balco Medical Centre has taken special steps to create a system which meets the special dietary requirements of cancer patients while not compromising with the taste. The food is prepared in a hygienic environment and a team of qualified nutritionists prepares the diet chart for every patient separately. Stringent quality control is followed at the Food & Beverage department and our facility being awarded an “Eat Right Campus” & 5-star hygiene rating by FSSAI is a testament to it. Psychological Counselling From diagnosis to treatment, people experiencing the cancer treatment journey often face emotional and physical trauma. Along with the individuals undergoing treatment, caregivers usually experience equal levels of stress, sometimes even more. At Balco Medical Centre, our qualified Clinical Psychologist provide counselling and psychotherapy to patients maintaining confidentiality. Mental Health Services Provided: • Family counselling and therapy for caregivers and attendants • Group sessions with patients for enhanced coping and support • De-addiction Clinic • Behaviour modification for children Dental Services The dental department at Balco Medical Centre goes beyond the scope of general dental treatment to include management of the mouth’s soft tissues and care for treatment side effects specific to oral cavity. Our trained and experienced dentist plays a vital role throughout cancer treatment by preventing and managing mouth sores, dental needs, oral pain and infections. For head and neck cancer patients and those on medication for bone damage, continuing dental care is very crucial. Rehabilitation Services Balco Medical Centre offers rehabilitation services, which includes a wide variety of therapies that are designed with the goal of helping patients to build strength, reduce stress, increase endurance, regain independence and sustain the energy needed to continue treatment and participate in daily activities. Physiotherapy Physiotherapists at Balco Medical Centre help with regaining movement and avoiding complications after cancer surgeries. They play a key role in assessing and rehabilitating balance, co-ordination, strength and mobility in patients whose cancer affects their nervous system. Physiotherapists teach and assist patients treated for various cancers like breast, prostate and haematological cancer to perform various exercises that improves mobility, fitness, muscular strength, and quality of life. They also consult to minimise the risk of developing lymphoedema, and, offer lymphoedema management. Speech & Swallowing Therapy Patients undergoing treatment for Head & Neck cancer experience trouble in both speech and swallowing, which in turn affect the patient's quality of life and ability to survive in the society. At Balco Medical Centre, we offer speech and swallowing therapy during the first 3 months post-treatment to get maximum benefits like understandability of speech, as judged by naive listeners, percent accuracy of production of consonant sounds and oropharyngeal swallow efficiency on liquid and paste.
